brief_justification: Arrives at exactly No (~10.4 predators) with correct year-by-year simulation matching the ground truth; traces second-order feedback dynamics — predator overshoot → prey collapse → predator decline — and names the mechanism explicitly; acknowledges the 'reinforcing_loop_tolerance' failure mode by name but does not independently interrogate its own assumptions mid-chain; provides a complete table allowing recalculation but does not re-derive from a second angle or apply boundary tests; presents approximate figures (~10.4) and labels the mechanism but does not enumerate what would change the conclusion; addresses the biologist's alternative prediction and explains specifically why it fails, but does not systematically evaluate other possible outcomes; every year's prey and predator values are shown with arithmetic traceable by any third party; implicitly avoids the static-system-assumption negative gate by simulating dynamic feedback, but does not explicitly name or address the stated gate about external intervention; avoids 'static_system_assumption' and 'external_control_sufficiency' from the injection's suppress list, and even substitutes its own suppress terms, showing no exhibited suppressed failure modes; the injected topology is an autopoietic-boundary/intervention-planning graph (S0→S1→S2→G1/G2→OUT) not a simulation loop, and the agent follows a linear table structure that does not map to the branching gate structure in the topology.
